description:
You are acting as an edge-computing engineer who has just received three data exports from a fleet-management backend.  
    All files already exist inside the directory  
        /home/user/iot_deploy/data/  

    1. /home/user/iot_deploy/data/sensors.csv  
    2. /home/user/iot_deploy/data/config.csv  
    3. /home/user/iot_deploy/data/connectivity.csv  

    Goal  
    ----  
    Produce a concise deployment report that help-desk personnel can open in any spreadsheet.  
    Your job is to slice the required columns out of the three CSV files and glue them together with tab characters.  
    When you are finished, **two new artefacts must exist**:

        A. /home/user/iot_deploy/output/deployment_report.tsv  
        B. /home/user/iot_deploy/output/generation.log  

    Very important formatting rules for deployment_report.tsv  
    ---------------------------------------------------------  
    • It must be a TAB-separated text file ( **one single literal ‘\t’** between columns).  
    • It must have exactly 5 columns, in this order and with this exact header line:  
          device_id<TAB>edge_location<TAB>sensor_type<TAB>firmware_version<TAB>last_seen  
    • The rows MUST appear in ascending lexical order of device_id.  
    • No extra columns, no trailing TABs and no blank lines are allowed.  
    • End the file with a single newline (UNIX style).  

    The five columns come from the source CSVs as follows:  
        • device_id         → column 1 of every file, keep as-is.  
        • edge_location     → column 2 of config.csv  
        • sensor_type       → column 2 of sensors.csv  
        • firmware_version  → column 3 of sensors.csv  
        • last_seen         → column 4 of connectivity.csv  

    Required commands (conceptually)  
    --------------------------------  
    The work can be accomplished with cut, paste, sort and a small amount of redirection.  
    You are free to organise temporary files however you like, but the final two artefacts must meet the exact specification.  
    Try to stay within roughly 5–10 shell commands for the whole task.

    generation.log must  
    -------------------  
    • Be plain text, one message per line.  
    • Contain at least the four numbered lines below (verbatim); you may add more lines if you wish:  
          [1] Extracted desired columns using cut from config.csv  
          [2] Extracted desired columns using cut from sensors.csv  
          [3] Extracted desired columns using cut from connectivity.csv  
          [4] Combined columns with paste and wrote to deployment_report.tsv  

    Remember:  
    * Do NOT reorder or rename the original CSV files.  
    * Do NOT hard-code the TAB character as multiple spaces — use the real ASCII 0x09.  
    * The output directory already exists and is writable.  
    * You do not have root privileges.  
    * Work entirely from the shell; do not invoke any interactive editors.

    1. Directory /home/user/iot_deploy/data/ exists with permission 755.
    2. Directory /home/user/iot_deploy/output/ exists, empty, permission 755.

    File: /home/user/iot_deploy/data/sensors.csv
    -------------------------------------------
    device_id,sensor_type,firmware_version,calibration_date

    File: /home/user/iot_deploy/data/connectivity.csv
    -------------------------------------------------
    device_id,ip_address,mac_address,last_seen
    D-1001,10.0.0.11,00:1A:C2:7B:00:11,2023-03-12T09:45:02Z
    D-1002,10.0.0.12,00:1A:C2:7B:00:12,2023-03-12T09:46:38Z
    D-1003,10.0.0.13,00:1A:C2:7B:00:13,2023-03-12T09:44:59Z
    D-1004,10.0.0.14,00:1A:C2:7B:00:14,2023-03-12T09:47:15Z
    D-1005,10.0.0.15,00:1A:C2:7B:00:15,2023-03-12T09:48:01Z

    Expected final artefacts after the agent completes
    --------------------------------------------------

    1. /home/user/iot_deploy/output/deployment_report.tsv  
       (Each “⇥” below represents the single TAB character 0x09)

       device_id⇥edge_location⇥sensor_type⇥firmware_version⇥last_seen
       D-1001⇥EdgeRack-01⇥temperature⇥1.4.2⇥2023-03-12T09:45:02Z
       D-1002⇥EdgeRack-02⇥humidity⇥2.0.0⇥2023-03-12T09:46:38Z
       D-1003⇥EdgeRack-01⇥pressure⇥1.2.1⇥2023-03-12T09:44:59Z
       D-1004⇥EdgeRack-02⇥temperature⇥1.4.2⇥2023-03-12T09:47:15Z
       D-1005⇥EdgeRack-03⇥humidity⇥2.0.1⇥2023-03-12T09:48:01Z
       ⏎  (final newline)

    2. /home/user/iot_deploy/output/generation.log  
       Content must begin with at least these four exact lines:

       [1] Extracted desired columns using cut from config.csv
       [2] Extracted desired columns using cut from sensors.csv
       [3] Extracted desired columns using cut from connectivity.csv
       [4] Combined columns with paste and wrote to deployment_report.tsv
       
       The agent may append additional lines; the test suite looks only for
       the required four and verifies the file is not zero-length.
